g.text: Tools that release managers can use to prepare and manage Airflow releases
Create a new version branch and update the default branches in main
Release production images to DockerHub (needs DockerHub permissions).
Generates content for issue to test the core release.
Generates content for issue to test the helm chart release.
Prepare CHANGELOG, README and COMMITS information for providers.
Update provider versions marked with '# use next version' comment.
Prepare sdist/whl distributions of Airflow Providers. Each provider directory is
keep in-tree generated files out of the artifact. See dev/breeze
Installs provider distributions that can be found in dist.
Verifies if all provider code is following expectations for providers.
Generates content for issue to test the release.
Generates tags for airflow provider releases.
Prepare sdist/whl distributions of Airflow Task SDK.
Generates content for issue to test airflow-ctl release.
Generates RST changelog for the airflowctl release and prepends it to
Prepare sdist/whl distributions of Apache Airflow Mypy.
Command to add back references for documentation to make it backward compatible.
Command to publish generated documentation to airflow-site
Generates pinned constraint files with all extras from pyproject.toml in parallel.
Update released constraints with manual changes.
[EXPERIMENTAL] Verify a release candidate for PMC voting.
Verify that all expected packages are present in Apache Airflow svn.
Check constraints against released versions of packages.
Merge production images in DockerHub based on digest files (needs DockerHub permissions).
